pattern: Using the first row and column as marker storage lets the zeroing be recorded and applied with only a couple of extra flags, avoiding any second matrix.
hints: If a cell is zero, its entire row and column must become zero, but you must decide all the zeros from the original matrix before overwriting anything.
You can remember which rows and columns to clear inside the matrix itself, by reusing the first row and first column as flag storage.

Track separately whether the first row and first column themselves contain a zero, mark the rest using row 0 and column 0, then zero the interior from those marks and finally the first row and column if flagged.

edge_inputs.description: zeros in the first row and column
zero at the top-left corner clears row and column 0
already all zeros stays all zeros

stress.description: random matrix seeded with a few zeros so rows and columns interact
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, 12)
    n = rng.randint(1, 12)
    matrix = [[(0 if rng.random() < 0.15 else rng.randint(-100, 100)) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [matrix]
stress.note: 15 percent zeros spreads zeroing across multiple rows and columns
